name: Sweet-And-Sour Spring Rolls - New Ama Cookbook
recipeInstructions:
1. Spray a large, nonstick skillet with vegetable oil spray and add 1/2 teaspoon of oil. Heat over medium-high heat for about 1 minute.
2. Add the cabbage and garlic to the skillet. Cook for 3 minutes, stirring constantly.
3. Stir in the green onions, bamboo shoots, soy sauce, and pepper. Cook for an additional 30 seconds, then remove from heat.
4. Place one sheet of phyllo dough on a damp towel. Brush with oil spray. Arrange the cabbage mixture along one edge of the dough, about 2 to 3 inches from the end.
5. Fold the dough over the filling and roll tightly, tucking in the sides. Set aside with the seam side down. Repeat with the remaining phyllo sheets and filling.
6. Heat a nonstick skillet or pan again with vegetable oil spray or oil. Cook the spring rolls for 6 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown.
7. Brush with sweet-and-sour sauce before serving.
Makes 4 servings, each containing 3 spring rolls; approximately 94 calories per serving (16% from fat), 2 grams of fat, 16 grams of carbohydrate, 3 grams of protein, 194 mg sodium, no cholesterol, and 28 mg calcium.
